name: Illinois Certified Nursing Assistant Certification
description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Illinois - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description: Illinois Department of Public Health, Health Care Worker Registry, 525 West Jefferson Street, Springfield, Illinois 62761

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (based on employment and in-service education)

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.

requiredDocuments.name: State Competency Exam Results
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

mainEntity.name: Who administers the Illinois CNA exam?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Illinois nurse aide exam is administered by SIU-C (Southern Illinois University Carbondale) via nurseaidetesting.com.

mainEntity.name: How do I transfer my CNA to Illinois?

acceptedAnswer.text: Submit the Out-of-State CNA Application on nurseaidetesting.com with a $25 money order payable to SIU-C. A written competency retest is usually required.

mainEntity.name: How do I verify an Illinois CNA?

acceptedAnswer.text: Search the Illinois Health Care Worker Registry (HCWR) at hcwr.illinois.gov or call (844) 789-3676 (M-F 8:30am-5pm CT).
